channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court to block the U.S. ban
item.description: TikTok on Monday asked the Supreme Court to step in on an emergency basis to block the federal law that would ban the popular platform in the United States unless its China-based parent company agreed to sell it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How Anyscale cofounder Robert Nishihara is smoothing AI’s complexities
item.description: As businesses reinvent workflows and products with AI, many such initiatives have stalled or failed to reach production. It can be really hard to implement the technology.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: It’s the end of the internet as we know it—and I feel fine
item.description: The internet feels like it’s falling apart. Not literally. Structurally, it’s sound. There are plenty of fiber optic cables lining ocean floors, cell towers looming above cityscapes, and server-filled data centers.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Scientists Discover Two Stars Orbiting Supermassive Black Hole in Milky Way Galaxy
item.description: Astronomers spot two stars whirling around each other near the supermassive black hole at the center of our galaxy, sparking further research and questions about their stability.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google Launches Gemini Code Assist Tools to Boost Developer Productivity
item.description: Google has launched new Gemini Code Assist tools designed to help developers improve their productivity from within an integrated development environment (IDE). The tools enable developers to stay in their coding interface while accessing related information from other applications or tools.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: House task force releases sweeping end-of-year report on AI
item.description: The House Task Force on Artificial Intelligence (AI) released its sweeping end-of-year report Tuesday, laying out a roadmap for Congress as it crafts policy surrounding the advancing technology. The 253-page report takes a deep dive into how the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Steps to protect yourself while shopping on your phone this holiday season
item.description: The holiday season sees a rise in mobile shopping scams. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you learn how to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Phillips 66 to build 88-acre solar project on Contra Costa County site
item.description: The Rodeo Renewable Energy Complex will consist of more than 70,000 solar modules that will generate about 60,000 Megawatt Hours per year of electricity, enough to power 23,000 electric vehicles for an entire year.
item.dc:creator: Nick Mcconnell, The Mercury News

item.title: Marvel Rivals' new Winter Celebration game mode will bring Splatoon-like chaos starring Jeff the Land Shark
item.description: NetEase has announced that a brand new, limited-time game mode will be coming to Marvel Rivals this week.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Laptop Wars: Intel vs Qualcomm on Arm-Based PCs
item.description: Intel co-CEO Michelle Johnston Holthaus claims a 'large percentage' of Arm-based PCs are returned, but Qualcomm disputes this, stating return rates are within industry norms.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: EU reveals sites for major AI factories across Europe
item.description: European Commission confirms seven locations for its next-generation AI Factories, more applications welcome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nothing indicates 'public safety risk' from drone sightings: Kirby
item.description: National security communications adviser John Kirby reiterated Monday there is no indication the recent drone sightings pose a risk to public safety.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: CD Projekt Red hints at Gwent returning in The Witcher 4 so there goes literally all of my spare time
item.description: Yep, Gwent is probably coming back in The Witcher 4 and I don't know whether to be excited or scared.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Smarter devops: How to avoid deployment horrors
item.description: Many devops organizations implement advanced CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and other automations to increase deployment frequency. In the State of DevOps Report 2023, 18% of respondents were classified as elite performers for being able to deploy on-demand with change lead times of less than a day.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How ride-hailing apps are replacing school buses
item.description: Ismael El-Amin was driving his daughter to school when a chance encounter gave him an idea for a new way to carpool. On the way across Chicago, El-Amin’s daughter spotted a classmate riding with her own dad as they drove to their selective public school on the city’s North Side.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: How to contact the Rhode Island cyberattack call center after data breach
item.description: Rhode Island officials urged residents to take immediate steps to protect themselves on Saturday, warning that the hackers could release personal data.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Corps of Engineers will look to save 150-year-old lighthouse from crumbling into the Hudson River
item.description: Efforts to save an imperiled 150-year-old lighthouse in New York are getting a boost with federal officials announcing new funding to study ways to protect the structure.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he has a 'warm spot' in his heart for TikTok as app faces ban
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday that he has a “warm spot” in his heart for TikTok as the popular social media app faces down a potential ban next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How old are Saturn's rings? Study suggests they could be 4.5 billion years old just like the planet
item.description: New research suggests that Saturn's rings may be older than they look — possibly as old as the planet.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Customized SAP platform serves as an advantage, not a challenge
item.description: You’d be hard-pressed to find an SAP shop that doesn’t rely on customizations to fine-tune the enterprise platform for specific business processes or industry requirements.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CompTIA introduces foundational tech certification
item.description: CompTIA has created a certification designed to provide individuals with broad foundational and digital skills that can be used to build a career in technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online ‘constantly,’ Pew report finds
item.description: YouTube was the single most popular platform teenagers used — 90% said they watched videos on the site, down slightly from 95% in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The race for data and AI expertise in government
item.description: Much is at stake to have a world-class public sector data and AI workforce amid the current global uncertainty and fierce talent competition. The post The race for data and AI expertise in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft introduces Phi-4, an AI model for advanced reasoning tasks
item.description: Microsoft has announced Phi-4 — a new AI model with 14 billion parameters — designed for complex reasoning tasks, including mathematics. Phi-4 excels in areas such as STEM question-answering and advanced problem-solving, surpassing similar models in performance.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scale AI sued by former worker alleging unlawful business practices
item.description: A new class action lawsuit alleges poor working conditions and exploitive behavior by AI data processing company Scale AI, saying that workers responsible for generating much of its product were mischaracterized by the company as independent contractors, rather than full employees.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AWS adds Graviton Savings Dashboard to help enterprises optimize infra costs
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has launched a dashboard for its Arm-based Graviton CPU that will measure savings opportunities in an effort to help enterprises further optimize their expenditure on subscribed infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell
item.description: [Task Force Lima] had made so much progress and had such concrete recommendations that we moved to stand up this cell for actual execution,” said Radha Plumb. The post DoD sunsets Task Force Lima, introduces AI rapid capabilities cell first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network
